contents.value:
```nginx
# Priority order (highest to lowest):
location = /exact { }        # 1. Exact match
location ^~ /images/ { }     # 2. Prefix priority (no regex check)
location ~ \.php$ { }        # 3. Regex (case-sensitive)
location ~* \.(jpg|png)$ { } # 4. Regex (case-insensitive)
location /prefix { }          # 5. Prefix match (longest wins)
location / { }                # 6. Default prefix
```
**location** matches request URIs. Nginx first checks exact (`=`), then prefix matches (longest wins), then regex (first match wins). `^~` stops regex checking.

```nginx
listen 80;                          # IPv4 port 80
listen [::]:80;                     # IPv6 port 80
listen 443 ssl http2;               # HTTPS with HTTP/2
listen 8080 default_server;         # default for this port
listen unix:/var/run/nginx.sock;    # Unix socket
listen 80 reuseport;                # kernel load balancing
listen 127.0.0.1:8080;              # localhost only
```
**listen** sets address, port, and options. Use both IPv4 and IPv6 (`[::]:80`) for dual-stack. `default_server` catches unmatched requests.

```nginx
# Request variables:
$host              # Host header (or server_name)
$uri               # Current URI (after rewrites)
$request_uri       # Original URI with query string
$args              # Query string
$request_method    # GET, POST, etc.
$scheme            # http or https
$server_port       # Port number

# Client variables:
$remote_addr            # Client IP
$binary_remote_addr     # Client IP (binary, for zones)
$http_user_agent        # User-Agent header
$http_referer           # Referer header
$http_cookie            # Cookie header
$http_<header>          # Any request header

# Proxy variables:
$proxy_add_x_forwarded_for  # X-Forwarded-For chain
$upstream_cache_status      # HIT, MISS, etc.
$upstream_response_time     # Backend response time
```
**Variables** are available in most directives. `$http_*` accesses any request header. Variables are evaluated lazily.

hovers.root_vs_alias:
## root vs alias

One of the most common nginx confusion points.

**root** — Appends the URI to the path:
```nginx
location /images/ {
    root /var/www;  # serves /var/www/images/logo.png
}
```

**alias** — Replaces the location path:
```nginx
location /images/ {
    alias /var/www/pics/;  # serves /var/www/pics/logo.png
}
```

**Key differences:**
- `root`: final path = root + URI
- `alias`: final path = alias + (URI - location)
- `alias` with regex location must capture and use `$1`
- `alias` must end with `/` when location ends with `/`

```nginx
# Regex with alias
location ~ ^/download/(.*)$ {
    alias /var/files/$1;  # must use captures
}
```
hovers.server_name:
## server_name

```nginx
server_name name ...;
```

Defines virtual server names. Nginx uses this to select which server block handles a request.

**Matching priority (first match wins):**
1. Exact name: `server_name example.com;`
2. Leading wildcard: `server_name *.example.com;`
3. Trailing wildcard: `server_name www.example.*;`
4. Regular expression: `server_name ~^(?<sub>.+)\.example\.com$;`
5. `default_server` flag on `listen`

```nginx
# Multiple names
server_name example.com www.example.com;

# Wildcard
server_name *.example.com;

# Regex with named capture
server_name ~^(?<subdomain>.+)\.example\.com$;

# Catch-all
server_name _;
```

**Tip:** Use `_` as a convention for the default catch-all server.

contents.value:
```nginx
log_format main '$remote_addr - $body_bytes_sent bytes';
```
**$body_bytes_sent** holds the number of bytes sent to the client (body only, excluding headers). Useful in log formats to track response sizes and bandwidth usage.

```nginx
proxy_pass http://backend$uri$is_args$args;
# $is_args is "?" if query string exists, empty otherwise
```
**$is_args** evaluates to `?` if the request has a query string, or empty string if not. Useful when building proxy URLs to conditionally include the `?` separator.

```nginx
proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
```
**$proxy_add_x_forwarded_for** is the `X-Forwarded-For` header from the client request with the `$remote_addr` appended. If no incoming header exists, it equals `$remote_addr`. Standard way to chain client IPs through proxies.

```nginx
fastcgi_param SCRIPT_FILENAME $realpath_root$fastcgi_script_name;
```
**$realpath_root** resolves `root` or `alias` to its real filesystem path, following all symlinks. Preferred over `$document_root` when using symlinked deployments (e.g., Capistrano, zero-downtime deploys).

```nginx
log_format timed '$remote_addr ... $request_time $upstream_response_time';
```
**$request_time** is the request processing time in seconds with millisecond resolution. Measured from when the first bytes were read from the client until the response was sent. Key metric for performance monitoring.

```nginx
log_format json '{"time":"$time_iso8601","status":$status}';
# Output: 2024-01-15T10:30:00+00:00
```
**$time_iso8601** is the local time in ISO 8601 format. Ideal for structured/JSON logging and log aggregation systems that expect standard timestamps.

```nginx
log_format main '$remote_addr [$time_local] "$request" $status';
# Output: 15/Jan/2024:10:30:00 +0000
```
**$time_local** is the local time in Common Log Format. The default time variable used in traditional access logs.

```nginx
log_format timed '... upstream=$upstream_response_time total=$request_time';
```
**$upstream_response_time** is the time spent receiving the response from the upstream server, in seconds with millisecond precision. Multiple values separated by commas if multiple upstreams were tried. Key for identifying slow backends.

```nginx
try_files $uri $uri/ /index.html;
rewrite ^/old(.*)$ /new$1 last;  # $uri reflects rewritten path
```
**$uri** is the current normalized request URI (decoded, without query string). Changes during internal rewrites and `try_files` processing. Use `$request_uri` for the original unmodified URI.

```nginx
worker_processes auto;  # one per CPU core (recommended)
worker_processes 4;     # fixed count
```
**worker_processes** sets the number of Nginx worker processes. `auto` detects the number of CPU cores. Each worker handles thousands of connections. Generally set to `auto` or match the CPU core count.

```nginx
worker_rlimit_nofile 65535;
```
**worker_rlimit_nofile** sets the maximum number of open file descriptors for worker processes. Should be at least `2 × worker_connections`. Set this to avoid 'Too many open files' errors under high load.

```nginx
tcp_nodelay on;
```
**tcp_nodelay** enables the `TCP_NODELAY` option, disabling Nagle's algorithm. Sends data immediately without waiting to coalesce small packets. Enabled by default for keep-alive connections. Important for low-latency websocket/streaming scenarios.

```nginx
keepalive_timeout 65;        # server timeout
keepalive_timeout 65 60;     # server 65s, client header 60s
```
**keepalive_timeout** sets how long an idle keep-alive connection stays open. First parameter is the server timeout; optional second parameter sets the `Keep-Alive: timeout=N` response header for the client.

```nginx
keepalive_requests 1000;  # max requests per keep-alive connection
```
**keepalive_requests** limits the maximum number of requests served through a single keep-alive connection. After this limit, the connection is closed. Default: 1000. Increase for high-traffic APIs.

```nginx
client_max_body_size 100m;
client_max_body_size 0;     # unlimited
```
**client_max_body_size** sets the maximum allowed size of the client request body. Returns `413 Request Entity Too Large` if exceeded. Set to `0` to disable checking. Applies to file uploads and POST data.

```nginx
client_body_timeout 60s;
```
**client_body_timeout** sets the timeout for reading the client request body. The timeout is set only between two successive read operations, not for the entire body transfer. Returns `408 Request Timeout` on expiry.

```nginx
client_header_timeout 60s;
```
**client_header_timeout** defines the timeout for reading the client request header. If the client does not transmit the complete header within this time, Nginx returns `408 Request Timeout`.

```nginx
index index.html index.htm index.php;
```
**index** defines the default files to serve when a directory is requested. Nginx checks files in order and serves the first one found. Can be set at `http`, `server`, or `location` level.

```nginx
return 301 https://$host$request_uri;  # permanent redirect
return 302 /maintenance.html;              # temporary redirect
return 200 'OK';                           # direct response
return 403;                                # forbidden
```
**return** sends a response with the specified status code and optional body/URL. For 301/302/303/307/308, the second argument is the redirect URL. For other codes, it's the response body. Faster than `rewrite` for simple redirects.

```nginx
log_format main '$remote_addr - $remote_user [$time_local] '
                '"$request" $status $body_bytes_sent '
                '"$http_referer" "$http_user_agent"';

log_format json escape=json '{"time":"$time_iso8601",'
               '"status":$status,"request":"$request"}';
```
**log_format** defines a named log format template using Nginx variables. Referenced by `access_log`. Supports `escape=json` for JSON logging. Only valid in the `http` block.

```nginx
proxy_http_version 1.1;
proxy_set_header Connection "";  # enable keepalive
```
**proxy_http_version** sets the HTTP protocol version for proxying. Use `1.1` for keep-alive connections to upstreams and WebSocket support. Default is `1.0` which closes connections after each request.

```nginx
proxy_buffering on;   # default, buffer upstream response
proxy_buffering off;  # stream directly to client (SSE, streaming)
```
**proxy_buffering** controls whether Nginx buffers the response from the upstream server. When `on`, Nginx reads the entire response before sending to the client. Turn `off` for Server-Sent Events, streaming, or real-time responses.

```nginx
proxy_buffer_size 4k;  # buffer for first part of response (headers)
```
**proxy_buffer_size** sets the size of the buffer for reading the first part of the upstream response (usually headers). If upstream headers exceed this, Nginx returns `502 Bad Gateway`. Increase for backends with large headers/cookies.

```nginx
proxy_cache_valid 200 302 10m;  # cache success for 10 min
proxy_cache_valid 404 1m;        # cache 404 for 1 min
proxy_cache_valid any 5m;        # cache everything for 5 min
```
**proxy_cache_valid** sets cache duration per HTTP status code. Overrides cache headers from upstream. Use `any` to match all status codes. Only applies when `proxy_cache` is active.

```nginx
proxy_cache_key "$scheme$request_method$host$request_uri";
proxy_cache_key "$host$uri$is_args$args";  # include query string
```
**proxy_cache_key** defines the key used for cache storage and lookup. Default: `$scheme$proxy_host$request_uri`. Customize to control cache granularity — e.g., include cookies or specific headers for user-specific caching.

```nginx
proxy_connect_timeout 60s;  # default: 60s
```
**proxy_connect_timeout** sets the timeout for establishing a connection with the upstream server. This does not limit the time for the entire response. If the connection cannot be established within this time, Nginx returns `502 Bad Gateway` or tries the next upstream.

```nginx
proxy_read_timeout 60s;  # default: 60s
proxy_read_timeout 300s; # for long-running APIs
```
**proxy_read_timeout** sets the timeout for reading a response from the upstream server. The timeout is between two successive read operations, not for the entire transfer. Increase for long-polling, slow APIs, or heavy computations.

```nginx
proxy_send_timeout 60s;  # default: 60s
```
**proxy_send_timeout** sets the timeout for transmitting a request to the upstream server. The timeout applies between two successive write operations. If the upstream does not receive any data within this time, the connection is closed.

```nginx
ssl_certificate /etc/letsencrypt/live/example.com/fullchain.pem;
```
**ssl_certificate** specifies the path to the SSL/TLS certificate file (PEM format). Should be the full chain (server cert + intermediates). Required for HTTPS server blocks.

```nginx
ssl_certificate_key /etc/letsencrypt/live/example.com/privkey.pem;
```
**ssl_certificate_key** specifies the path to the private key file for the SSL certificate. File should be readable only by the Nginx master process (`chmod 600`).

```nginx
ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3;  # recommended minimum
```
**ssl_protocols** specifies the TLS protocol versions to enable. Disable TLSv1 and TLSv1.1 for security. TLSv1.2 provides broad compatibility; TLSv1.3 offers the best performance and security.

```nginx
ssl_ciphers 'ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384';
ssl_ciphers HIGH:!aNULL:!MD5;  # simpler approach
```
**ssl_ciphers** defines the OpenSSL cipher suite list for TLS connections. Use `ssl_prefer_server_ciphers on` to enforce server cipher order. With TLSv1.3, ciphers are fixed and this directive is ignored.

```nginx
ssl_prefer_server_ciphers on;
```
**ssl_prefer_server_ciphers** tells Nginx to use the server's cipher preference order instead of the client's. Ensures stronger ciphers are preferred. Enable alongside a curated `ssl_ciphers` list.

```nginx
ssl_dhparam /etc/nginx/dhparam.pem;
# Generate: openssl dhparam -out /etc/nginx/dhparam.pem 2048
```
**ssl_dhparam** specifies the Diffie-Hellman parameters file for DHE ciphers. Provides forward secrecy. Generate with `openssl dhparam`. Use at least 2048-bit parameters.

```nginx
ssl_session_cache shared:SSL:10m;  # 10MB shared across workers
# ~40,000 sessions per 1MB
```
**ssl_session_cache** configures the TLS session cache. `shared:NAME:SIZE` creates a cache shared between all worker processes. Reduces CPU overhead by reusing TLS sessions. Set size based on expected concurrent connections.

```nginx
ssl_session_timeout 1d;  # cache sessions for 1 day
```
**ssl_session_timeout** sets how long a TLS session is kept in the session cache. Longer times reduce TLS handshakes for returning clients. Default: 5m. Common production value: 1d.

```nginx
ssl_session_tickets off;  # disable for forward secrecy
```
**ssl_session_tickets** enables/disables TLS session tickets (RFC 5077). While improving performance, tickets can compromise forward secrecy unless ticket keys are rotated regularly. Disable for maximum security.

```nginx
ssl_trusted_certificate /etc/letsencrypt/live/example.com/chain.pem;
```
**ssl_trusted_certificate** specifies the CA certificate chain for verifying OCSP responses (used with `ssl_stapling_verify on`) and client certificates. Must contain the full chain from server cert to root CA.

```nginx
limit_req zone=api burst=20 nodelay;
limit_req zone=api burst=5;  # with delay (default)
```
**limit_req** applies request rate limiting defined by `limit_req_zone`. `burst` allows short bursts beyond the rate. `nodelay` processes burst requests immediately; without it, excess requests are delayed. Returns `503` when limit exceeded.

```nginx
limit_conn_zone $binary_remote_addr zone=conn:10m;
```
**limit_conn_zone** defines a shared memory zone for concurrent connection limiting. The key determines how connections are grouped (typically by client IP). Used with `limit_conn` to restrict simultaneous connections per key.

```nginx
limit_conn conn 10;  # max 10 concurrent connections per IP
limit_conn_status 429;
```
**limit_conn** restricts the number of concurrent connections from a single key (defined by `limit_conn_zone`). Returns `503` by default when exceeded (override with `limit_conn_status`). Effective against slow-connection DDoS attacks.

```nginx
resolver 8.8.8.8 8.8.4.4 valid=300s;
resolver 127.0.0.1 valid=30s ipv6=off;  # local DNS
```
**resolver** configures DNS servers for resolving upstream hostnames, OCSP responders, and other dynamic names. `valid` sets the TTL override. Required for `ssl_stapling`, variables in `proxy_pass`, and dynamic upstreams.

```nginx
fastcgi_index index.php;
```
**fastcgi_index** sets the filename appended to a URI ending with `/` when proxying to a FastCGI server. Used to construct the `SCRIPT_FILENAME` for directory requests.

```nginx
gzip_comp_level 5;  # range: 1-9
# 1 = fastest/least compression
# 9 = slowest/best compression
# 5-6 = good balance
```
**gzip_comp_level** sets the gzip compression level (1-9). Higher values compress more but use more CPU. Levels 4-6 provide the best balance between compression ratio and CPU overhead. Default: 1.

```nginx
gzip_types text/plain text/css application/json application/javascript
           text/xml application/xml text/javascript image/svg+xml;
```
**gzip_types** specifies which MIME types to compress in addition to `text/html` (which is always compressed). Add all text-based types. Don't compress already-compressed formats (images, video, archives).

```nginx
gzip_min_length 256;  # don't compress tiny responses
```
**gzip_min_length** sets the minimum response size to apply gzip compression. Responses smaller than this are sent uncompressed. Prevents overhead on tiny responses where compression adds more bytes than it saves. Default: 20.

```nginx
gzip_proxied any;       # compress all proxied responses
gzip_proxied no-cache no-store private expired auth;
```
**gzip_proxied** controls whether responses from proxied requests are compressed, based on the `Via` header presence and cache-control headers. `any` compresses all proxied responses.

```nginx
gzip_vary on;
# Adds: Vary: Accept-Encoding
```
**gzip_vary** adds a `Vary: Accept-Encoding` response header when gzip is active. Essential for correct caching behavior — ensures CDNs and proxies cache compressed and uncompressed versions separately.
